metadata.description: StarDist is a deep learning method for star-convex object detection in 2D and 3D microscopy images, particularly suited for cell and nucleus segmentation.

params.name: Probability Threshold
params.tooltip: Threshold for pixel-wise object probability mask. Higher values lead to fewer detected objects with higher confidence.

params.name: NMS Threshold
params.tooltip: Intersection over Union (IoU) threshold for non-maximum suppression. Higher values allow for more overlapping objects.

params.name: Normalize Min Percentile
params.tooltip: Lower percentile for image normalization. Images are normalized to this percentile value before prediction.

params.name: Normalize Max Percentile
params.tooltip: Upper percentile for image normalization. Images are normalized to this percentile value before prediction.

params.name: Number of Tiles
params.tooltip: Number of tiles for tiled prediction to reduce memory consumption for large images. Specify as tuple (e.g., (2, 2) for 2x2 tiling).

params.name: Normalize Image
params.tooltip: Whether to normalize the input image before prediction. Recommended for most cases.

params.name: Scale
params.tooltip: Optional scaling factor for the input image. Can be used to match the resolution of training data.

params.name: Channel Index
params.tooltip: Select which channel StarDist should use. -1=use original image as-is, 0=first channel, 1=second channel, etc. Ignored for model variants that consume multi-channel images directly.
